org.arakhne.neteditor.io.graphml

Class GraphMLWriter

  • java.lang.Object
    • org.arakhne.neteditor.io.AbstractNetEditorWriter
      • org.arakhne.neteditor.io.xml.AbstractXMLWriter
        • org.arakhne.neteditor.io.graphml.GraphMLWriter
  • All Implemented Interfaces:
    GraphMLConstants, NetEditorWriter, XMLConstants

    public class GraphMLWriter
extends AbstractXMLWriter
implements GraphMLConstants
    This class permits to export the graph-model into the GraphML format.

    GraphML is a comprehensive and easy-to-use file format for graphs. It consists of a language core to describe the structural properties of a graph and a flexible extension mechanism to add application-specific data.

    Since:
    16.0
    Version:
    17.0 2013-10-23 21:03:04
    Author:
    Stéphane GALLAND
    See Also:
    "http://graphml.graphdrawing.org/"
    Maven Group Id:
    org.arakhne.neteditor
    Maven Artifact Id:
    neteditor-io

      • isWriteSVGDrawings

        public boolean isWriteSVGDrawings()
        Replies if this writer is also writing SVG drawing of the nodes and the edges.
        Returns:
        true if the SVG drawing are written; false otherwise.

      • setWriteSVGDrawings

        public void setWriteSVGDrawings(boolean drawSvg)
        Set if this writer is also writing SVG drawing of the nodes and the edges.
        Parameters:
        drawSvg - is true if the SVG drawing are written; false otherwise.

      • createGraphDOM

        protected Element createGraphDOM(Map<UUID,? extends Graph<?,?,?,?>> graphs,
                     Collection<? extends Figure> figures,
                     Progression progression)
                          throws IOException
        Create the DOM of the content.
        Specified by:
        createGraphDOM in class AbstractXMLWriter
        Parameters:
        graphs - are the graphs to output.
        figures - are the figures to output. The list is ordered from the front layer to the background layer.
        progression - is the task progression.
        Returns:
        the DOM element.
        Throws:
        IOException
